name: Davis Truck & Bus Of Mi LLC

fit_notes: Unclear if operator or vehicle service/dealer. Marshall Public Schools (Calhoun County, MI) appears to run own transportation. Low-priority unless verified as a contract operator; geography is Summit Mobility footprint.

name: Bruk Transportation LLC
recent_news: Registered with NPI #1992395446 as Non-Emergency Medical Transport Van provider; listings show Hanover NH (50 S Main St) and Piermont NH addresses. No school-bus contract evidence found.
fit_notes: Appears to be a NEMT van operator (medical transport) rather than school-bus contractor. Limited fit for Summit unless they hold school/SPED contracts; warrants direct outreach to confirm services. Upper Valley NH geography.

name: California Sightseeing Tours
recent_news: Listed on Yelp at 2270 Jerrold Ave, San Francisco (415-647-9012). Name and city positioning indicate tourist/sightseeing motor-coach charter, not school-bus operator.
fit_notes: Appears to be a tour/charter motor-coach business serving San Francisco tourism, not a yellow-bus K-12 contractor. Poor fit for Summit tuck-in unless they hold sideline school/activity contracts; classification likely a false positive in source list.

name: Havoc Inc Which Will Do Bus
recent_news: California entity registered at 1592 London Cir, Benicia CA 94510 with Legalinc Registered Agents as the agent. Name 'Havoc Inc Which Will Do Business As ...' indicates a DBA structure with the operating name truncated in the source list. No school-bus contract or fleet info found.
fit_notes: Entity name truncation ('Which Will Do Bus[iness As]') means the real operating brand is unknown; record is likely a shell/DBA filing. Residential address in Benicia suggests micro-operator. Very low priority absent identification of the actual DBA.

name: D Verrett Bus Trnsprtn LLC
districts_or_customers: Likely New Orleans-area charter/public schools (Algiers/West Bank)
recent_news: No M&A or news found. Operates from 4480 Gen DeGaulle Dr (Algiers/West Bank, New Orleans). NOLA has a fragmented school-bus vendor market across charter networks; small minority-owned operators are common after Katrina-era system rebuild.
fit_notes: Small family-owned LA school-bus contractor likely serving New Orleans charter network; sub-scale (~12 employees) but founder 'Sr' designation hints at succession potential. Geographic fit is opportunistic (LA not core Summit state); only attractive as part of a NOLA-area rollup.
